title: GitHub Copilot Tool Tips
description: A curated collection of tools, extensions, and resources that enhance your GitHub Copilot experience
intro: Unlock the full potential of GitHub Copilot with these community-built tools, VS Code extensions, and curated resource collections. Whether you want to track your token usage, gain deeper AI insights inside your editor, or discover new ways to integrate Copilot into your workflow, these tool tips will help you get more out of your AI pair programmer.

tools.name: Copilot Token Tracker
tools.description: A VS Code extension that shows your daily and monthly GitHub Copilot estimated token usage in the status bar. Track consumption patterns, view usage analysis dashboards, measure your Copilot Fluency Score, and optionally sync data across devices with an Azure Storage backend for team-wide reporting.

tools.name: VS Code Copilot Insights
tools.description: A Visual Studio Code extension that provides detailed insights into your GitHub Copilot interactions directly within your editor. Track acceptance rates, view suggestion statistics, and understand how effectively you are using Copilot in your daily development workflow.

tools.name: Awesome Copilot
tools.description: A community-maintained collection of awesome resources, tools, and tips for GitHub Copilot. Curated by GitHub, this repository brings together the best extensions, guides, integrations, and learning materials to help you master AI-assisted development.

tools.name: Context7
tools.description: An MCP server that provides up-to-date library documentation directly to AI coding assistants. Context7 resolves the problem of outdated training data by fetching the latest documentation for any library, ensuring your AI pair programmer gives accurate and current suggestions.

tools.name: Playwright MCP
tools.description: The official Microsoft Playwright MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that enables AI agents to interact with web browsers. Automate browser testing, perform web scraping, and build AI-powered end-to-end test workflows with structured browser control.

tools.name: Spec Kit
tools.description: An open-source toolkit by GitHub for Spec-Driven Development. Instead of vibe coding from scratch, Spec Kit lets you focus on product scenarios and predictable outcomes by turning specifications into executable implementations through a structured multi-step workflow with your AI coding agent.
